signals.title: OSS-CRS: Liberating AIxCC Cyber Reasoning Systems for Real-World Open-Source Security

content.abstract: DARPA's AI Cyber Challenge (AIxCC) showed that cyber reasoning systems (CRSs) can go beyond vulnerability discovery to autonomously confirm and patch bugs: seven teams built such systems and open-sourced them after the competition. Yet all seven open-sourced CRSs remain largely unusable outside their original teams, each bound to the competition cloud infrastructure. This paper presents OSS-CRS, a framework that liberates these systems from competition constraints, enabling real-world deployment for open-source software security.

signals.impact_analysis: DARPA-backed autonomous vulnerability patching systems transitioning from competition to real-world deployment represents a paradigm shift in cybersecurity. Seven independent CRS implementations now available for open-source security could dramatically accelerate vulnerability response times from weeks to minutes.
cross_impacts: Directly enables automated cyber defense infrastructure
Reduces dependency on human security analysts for routine patches
Could create arms race dynamics with AI-powered offensive tools
monitoring_indicators: Number of real-world patches generated by CRS systems

signals.title: Lattice: A Post-Quantum Settlement Layer for Cryptocurrency

content.abstract: We present Lattice (L, ticker: LAT), a peer-to-peer electronic cash system designed as a post-quantum settlement layer for the era of quantum computing. Lattice combines three independent defense vectors: hardware resilience through RandomX CPU-only proof-of-work, network resilience through LWMA-1 per-block difficulty adjustment, and cryptographic resilience through post-quantum lattice-based signatures and key exchange.

signals.impact_analysis: First comprehensive post-quantum cryptocurrency settlement layer combining hardware, network, and cryptographic resilience. As quantum computing advances threaten existing blockchain cryptography, this represents early infrastructure preparation for post-quantum financial systems.
cross_impacts: Signals urgency of quantum threat timeline to financial infrastructure
May accelerate NIST post-quantum standard adoption in fintech
Creates template for quantum-resistant distributed systems
monitoring_indicators: Adoption of lattice-based cryptography in financial systems

signals.title: SplitAgent: Privacy-Preserving Enterprise-Cloud AI Agent Architecture

content.abstract: Enterprise adoption of cloud-based AI agents faces a fundamental privacy dilemma: leveraging powerful cloud models requires sharing sensitive data, while local processing limits capability. Current agent frameworks like MCP and A2A assume complete data sharing, making them unsuitable for enterprise environments with confidential information. We present SplitAgent, a privacy-preserving distributed architecture that splits agent computation between enterprise-local and cloud components.

signals.impact_analysis: Directly addresses the #1 enterprise AI adoption blocker: data privacy. By splitting computation between local and cloud, SplitAgent enables enterprises to use powerful cloud AI while keeping sensitive data on-premise. This architectural pattern could become standard for regulated industries (healthcare, finance, legal).
cross_impacts: Enables regulated industries to adopt AI agents
Challenges MCP/A2A frameworks to add privacy layers
May influence EU AI Act compliance architecture
monitoring_indicators: Enterprise AI agent adoption rates in regulated sectors
Regulatory guidance on AI data residency

signals.title: Agentic Critical Training: Beyond Imitation Learning for LLM Agents

content.abstract: Training large language models (LLMs) as autonomous agents often begins with imitation learning, but it only teaches agents what to do without understanding why: agents never contrast successful actions against suboptimal alternatives and thus lack awareness of action quality. This paper introduces Agentic Critical Training, which teaches LLM agents to understand not just what to do but why, through contrastive reasoning over action quality.

signals.impact_analysis: Addresses fundamental limitation of current AI agent training (imitation without understanding). This contrastive approach to agent training could significantly improve reliability and error recovery in autonomous AI systems, moving beyond simple pattern mimicry to causal understanding of actions.
cross_impacts: Could accelerate deployment of AI agents in high-stakes domains
Reduces failure modes from blind imitation
Influences AI safety through better action understanding
monitoring_indicators: Adoption of contrastive training in production AI agents
Error rate reduction in deployed AI systems

signals.title: Visual Self-Fulfilling Alignment: Safety-Oriented Personas for Multimodal LLMs

content.abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) face safety misalignment, where visual inputs enable harmful outputs. This paper introduces Visual Self-Fulfilling Alignment, a novel approach that shapes safety-oriented personas via threat-related images, without requiring explicit safety labels or contrastive data.

signals.impact_analysis: Novel approach to multimodal AI safety that doesn't require expensive safety labeling. As multimodal AI (GPT-4V, Gemini, Claude) becomes dominant, visual jailbreak attacks are a critical vulnerability. This self-reinforcing alignment method could dramatically reduce the cost of making multimodal models safer.
cross_impacts: Reduces multimodal AI safety alignment costs
Addresses visual jailbreak attack vectors
Could influence regulatory standards for multimodal AI safety testing
monitoring_indicators: Regulatory requirements for multimodal AI testing

signals.title: Open-Source Platform for Autonomous Laparoscopic Surgery

content.abstract: Autonomous robot-assisted surgery demands reliable, high-precision platforms that strictly adhere to the safety and kinematic constraints of minimally invasive procedures. This paper presents an open-source robotics research platform for autonomous laparoscopic surgery, overcoming limitations of existing da Vinci Research Kit-based systems.

signals.impact_analysis: Open-sourcing autonomous surgical robotics democratizes access to surgical AI research beyond institutions that can afford da Vinci systems ($2M+). This could accelerate the timeline for autonomous surgery from current teleoperated paradigm to semi-autonomous and eventually fully autonomous procedures.
cross_impacts: Lowers barrier to surgical robotics research globally
Challenges Intuitive Surgical's monopoly on research platforms
Accelerates regulatory framework development for autonomous surgery
monitoring_indicators: Number of institutions adopting the open-source platform
FDA guidance on autonomous surgical systems
Clinical trial initiation for autonomous surgical procedures

signals.title: Clinical Feasibility Study of Conversational Diagnostic AI in Primary Care

content.abstract: LLM-based AI systems have shown promise for patient-facing diagnostic and management conversations in simulated settings. This paper reports a prospective, single-arm feasibility study of an LLM-based conversational diagnostic AI system deployed in a real ambulatory primary care clinic, with rigorous safety oversight.

signals.impact_analysis: First prospective clinical feasibility study of conversational diagnostic AI in real primary care settings (not simulated). This transition from lab to clinic is a critical milestone for AI-assisted healthcare. Success could reshape primary care delivery models globally, addressing physician shortage crisis.
cross_impacts: Could accelerate regulatory pathways for diagnostic AI
Addresses global physician shortage through AI augmentation
Raises questions about liability in AI-assisted diagnosis
monitoring_indicators: Regulatory approval applications for diagnostic AI

signals.title: SCAFFOLD-CEGIS: Preventing Security Degradation in LLM Code Refinement

content.abstract: The application of LLMs to code generation has evolved from one-shot generation to iterative refinement, yet the evolution of security throughout iteration remains insufficiently understood. This paper reveals the iterative refinement paradox: specification drift during multiple rounds of LLM code refinement systematically introduces security vulnerabilities that were absent in initial generations.

signals.impact_analysis: Reveals critical vulnerability in AI-assisted coding workflows: iterative refinement (used by GitHub Copilot, Cursor, etc.) systematically degrades code security over multiple rounds. This 'iterative refinement paradox' threatens the entire AI coding assistant industry's security posture.
cross_impacts: Challenges security assumptions of AI coding tools (Copilot, Cursor, Devin)
May trigger regulatory requirements for AI-generated code auditing
Creates demand for security-aware code generation architectures
stakeholders: Software developers using AI coding assistants
Software supply chain security standards bodies
monitoring_indicators: AI coding tool security audit adoption rates
Regulatory guidance on AI-generated code in critical systems

signals.title: SlowBA: Efficiency Backdoor Attack Against VLM-Based GUI Agents

content.abstract: Modern VLM-based GUI agents are expected to execute actions accurately and with low latency. While existing research focuses on manipulating action correctness, security risks related to response efficiency remain largely unexplored. This paper introduces SlowBA, a novel efficiency backdoor attack that targets VLM-based GUI agents by degrading their response time rather than their accuracy.

signals.impact_analysis: Novel attack vector against AI GUI agents (like Computer Use): instead of corrupting outputs, attackers degrade response time, making agents unusable. This 'slow-kill' approach is harder to detect than accuracy attacks and could undermine trust in AI agent deployment for critical workflows.
cross_impacts: New threat category for AI agent security assessment
Impacts deployment timeline of autonomous computer-using agents
Requires new defense metrics beyond accuracy
stakeholders: AI agent developers (Anthropic Computer Use, OpenAI)
monitoring_indicators: Security certification requirements for AI agents

signals.title: Cybersecurity AI: Hacking Consumer Robots in the AI Era

content.abstract: Is robot cybersecurity broken by AI? Consumer robots -- from autonomous lawnmowers to powered exoskeletons and window cleaners -- are rapidly entering homes and workplaces, yet their security remains rooted in assumptions of specialized attacker expertise. This paper presents evidence that Generative AI fundamentally changes the threat landscape by enabling non-expert attackers to hack consumer robots.

signals.impact_analysis: Demonstrates that generative AI democratizes robot hacking, enabling non-expert attackers to compromise consumer robots (lawnmowers, exoskeletons, window cleaners). As consumer robot market grows ($30B+ by 2028), this paper quantifies how AI lowers the barrier to physical-world attacks.
cross_impacts: Consumer robot security certification urgently needed
Physical safety implications (weaponized home robots)
Insurance and liability framework gaps for robot hacking
monitoring_indicators: Security certification standards for consumer robots

signals.title: Carbon-aware Market Participation for Building Energy Management Systems

content.abstract: Tackling climate change requires rapid and deep decarbonization of electric power systems. While energy management systems (EMSs) play a central role, conventional EMSs focus mainly on economic efficiency and often overlook the environmental impact. This paper proposes a unified, carbon-aware market participation framework for building energy management.

signals.impact_analysis: Shifts building energy management from pure cost optimization to carbon-aware optimization. With buildings accounting for ~40% of global energy consumption, carbon-aware EMS could significantly accelerate decarbonization by aligning economic incentives with emissions reduction in real-time energy markets.
cross_impacts: Enables carbon-aware building operations at scale
Aligns with EU/US building emissions regulations
Creates new carbon market participation models for commercial buildings
monitoring_indicators: Carbon-aware EMS adoption in commercial buildings
Carbon market integration standards for buildings

signals.title: How Far Can Unsupervised RLVR Scale LLM Training?

content.abstract: Unsupervised reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (URLVR) offers a pathway to scale LLM training beyond the supervision bottleneck by deriving rewards without ground truth labels. This paper investigates the scaling limits and potential of unsupervised RLVR for training large language models.

signals.impact_analysis: Explores whether LLM training can scale without human supervision bottleneck. If unsupervised RLVR proves effective at scale, it removes the key constraint on LLM capability growth: the cost and availability of human preference data. This could fundamentally change the economics of AI model training.
cross_impacts: Could eliminate human labeling as LLM training bottleneck
Accelerates AI capability growth curve
Raises AI safety concerns about unsupervised self-improvement
monitoring_indicators: AI safety governance responses to self-supervised training

signals.title: Aligning to Illusions: Choice Blindness in Human and AI Feedback

content.abstract: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) assumes annotator preferences reflect stable internal states. This paper challenges this through three experiments: 91% of surreptitiously swapped preferences go undetected, extending choice blindness to AI alignment. This reveals fundamental epistemological vulnerability in current AI alignment methods.

signals.impact_analysis: Demonstrates that 91% of swapped human preferences go undetected in RLHF pipelines, revealing that the foundational assumption of RLHF — stable human preferences — is empirically false. This 'choice blindness' problem undermines the theoretical basis of current AI alignment approaches used by all major AI labs.
cross_impacts: Challenges theoretical foundation of RLHF-based AI alignment
May shift alignment research toward non-preference-based methods
Implications for democratic AI governance (citizens can't reliably express AI preferences)
stakeholders: AI labs using RLHF (OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepMind)

signals.title: X-AVDT: Audio-Visual Cross-Attention for Robust Deepfake Detection

content.abstract: The surge of highly realistic synthetic videos produced by contemporary generative systems has significantly increased the risk of malicious use. This paper introduces X-AVDT, an audio-visual cross-attention framework for robust deepfake detection that exploits internal cross-attention mechanisms of generators themselves.

signals.impact_analysis: Novel deepfake detection approach using generators' own internal mechanisms against them. Audio-visual cross-modal detection is more robust than visual-only methods. Critical for combating election disinformation, financial fraud, and celebrity impersonation at scale.
cross_impacts: Strengthens media authentication infrastructure
Relevant to 2026 election integrity
Creates new market for enterprise deepfake detection
monitoring_indicators: Regulatory mandates for synthetic media detection

signals.title: MetaWorld-X: Hierarchical World Modeling for Humanoid Loco-Manipulation

content.abstract: Learning natural, stable, and compositionally generalizable whole-body control policies for humanoid robots performing simultaneous locomotion and manipulation remains a fundamental challenge. This paper presents MetaWorld-X, a hierarchical world modeling approach using VLM-orchestrated experts for humanoid loco-manipulation.

signals.impact_analysis: VLM-orchestrated humanoid robot control combining locomotion and manipulation simultaneously. This bridges the gap between current single-task robot policies and the generalist humanoid vision (Figure, Tesla Optimus, 1X). Hierarchical world models could be the key architecture for commercial humanoid deployment.
cross_impacts: Advances commercial humanoid robot timeline
VLM integration creates new robotics paradigm
Impacts labor market projections for physical work
